title: Workflow engine initial development — requirements and implementation

comment:
QA Pre-Review: CR-110 — COMPLIANT. Recommend for approval.

FRONTMATTER VERIFIED:
- title: "Workflow engine initial development — requirements and implementation" (present, non-empty)
- revision_summary: "Initial draft" (present; acceptable for v0.1)

PLACEHOLDER CHECK:
- No {{double-curly}} placeholders found in pre-approved content
- No TBD, TODO, FIXME, or XXX found
- Execution-phase [SQUARE_BRACKET] placeholders correctly present in Sections 10-11

DOCUMENT STRUCTURE (all 12 required sections present per TEMPLATE-CR.md):
1. Purpose — present, clear
2. Scope (2.1 Context, 2.2 Changes Summary, 2.3 Files Affected) — all subsections present
3. Current State — present
4. Proposed State — present
5. Change Description (5.1-5.4) — present, includes containment boundary table
6. Justification — present, four rationale points
7. Impact Assessment (7.1-7.3) — present
8. Testing Summary — present, explains pre-qualification testing approach
9. Implementation Plan — present, 7 steps
10. Execution — EI table with 7 items, properly formatted
11. Execution Summary — execution-phase placeholder
12. References — SOPs 001, 002, 005, 006; CR-109, CR-108; session notes

SECTIONS 7.4/7.5 OMISSION:
Sections 7.4 (Development Controls) and 7.5 (Qualified State Continuity) are intentionally omitted. This is justified: the WFE system has no existing qualified state, no CI pipeline, no merge gate, and no RTM. The template instructs these sections be included "when the CR modifies controlled code" — but the WFE system is not yet under SDLC governance with a qualified baseline. Including these sections would require populating nonexistent values (current RS version, qualified release). The omission is logically consistent with the CR's explicit pre-qualification scope.

EI COVERAGE:
All 7 implementation plan steps map 1:1 to EIs in the execution table. No gaps.

EI-4 BREADTH:
EI-4 ("Implement workflow engine driven by RS requirements") is intentionally broad, authorizing the entire development effort as a single EI. This is appropriate given the containment boundaries in Section 5.4 and the pre-qualification nature of the work.

INTERNAL CONSISTENCY:
- Section 2.3 (Files Affected) aligns with Section 7.1 (Files Affected table)
- Section 4 (Proposed State) aligns with Section 5 (Change Description)
- Section 9 (Implementation Plan) aligns with Section 10 (EI table)
- RS DRAFT status stated consistently in Sections 4, 5.2, and 8

CONTAINMENT BOUNDARIES (Section 5.4):
Five explicit constraints defined in table format: code only in qms-workflow-engine/, QMS documents only the draft RS, no changes to existing submodules or controlled documents, no qualification attempted. Clear and enforceable.

REFERENCES: Appropriate SOPs and predecessor CRs cited.
FORMATTING: Consistent throughout. No inconsistencies.
TYPOS/ERRORS: None found.

REVIEWER ASSIGNMENT: No TU assignment warranted. This CR does not affect any existing Flow State domain code (UI, scene, sketch, simulation). The workflow engine is a new, independent system.
